- session_shutdown calls poller.stop()
- Test: no duplicate intervals after session switch

title: Extension structure: package layout and entry point
description:
Set up the extension directory structure: pi-threads/index.ts (entry), pi-threads/thread-manager.ts, pi-threads/tools/, pi-threads/types.ts. Extension registers all tools and hooks into session_start for resumption.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Extension directory at ~/.pi/agent/extensions/pi-threads/ with index.ts entry
- types.ts exports all protocol types (InterAgentCommunication, SubAgentActivityEvent, ThreadMeta, etc.)
- thread-manager.ts exports ThreadManager class with spawn/wait/list/interrupt/close/resume
- tools/ contains individual tool files (spawn, wait, send, list, interrupt, close)
- index.ts registers all tools and hooks session_start for resumption
- Extension loads cleanly without errors in pi
close_reason: Extension scaffold: ThreadManager, 6 tools, session_start hook, 11 tests passing

title: Fix: thread picker shortcuts alt+left/alt+right collide with built-in tree navigation
description:
## Problem

pi-threads registers alt+left and alt+right as global shortcuts for previous/next thread session in thread-picker.ts (lines 196-208). These override pi's built-in session tree keybindings:

| Shortcut | Built-in pi binding | pi-threads override |
|---|---|---|
| alt+left | app.tree.foldOrUp (fold node / navigate up) | Previous thread session |
| alt+right | app.tree.unfoldOrDown (unfold node / navigate down) | Next thread session |

When pi-threads is loaded, alt+left/alt+right no longer navigate the session tree — they fire sendUserMessage("/threads-prev") / sendUserMessage("/threads-next") instead. Users lose core tree navigation.

## Related

- pi-threads-nkd (P1 bug) — sendUserMessage throwing during streaming from the same shortcut handlers. Even after that fix, the keybinding collision remains.

## Fix Options

A. **Different keys** — alt+, / alt+. or ctrl+[/ctrl+], avoiding pi's tree bindings
B. **Commands only** — keep /threads-prev and /threads-next as commands, drop global shortcuts. Users can bind them manually via pi keybinding config.
C. **Scoped** — two-key chord, or only active when thread picker widget has focus

title: Persistence layer: thread session schema and metadata entries
description:
Design and implement the session entry types and protocol definitions — all custom types built on Pi primitives (pi.appendEntry, pi.sendMessage, SessionManager), not native Pi internals.

1) thread_meta custom entry (child session): parent_id, thread_id, thread_name, depth, task, agent_type. Written as the first custom entry after the session header. This is THE marker that distinguishes a thread session from a regular user session when scanning with SessionManager.list().
2) thread_spawned custom entry (parent session): thread_id, thread_name, parent_id, depth, agent_type. Written for durable tree reconstruction via pi.appendEntry() — NOT the same as the inline transcript event (see below).
3) thread_completed custom entry: status (completed/error/aborted/closed), usage, exit_code. Single entry type for all terminal states — no separate 'thread_closed' entry.
4) InterAgentCommunication envelope: { author, recipient, content } — prepended to all inter-agent prompts as structured text: '[From <author> to <recipient>]: <content>'
5) SubAgentActivityEvent — via pi.sendMessage with customType (thread_spawned, thread_send, thread_wait, thread_interrupted, thread_closed) for inline TUI transcript rendering. These are separate from the pi.appendEntry() durable tree entries.
6) Dual-write pattern: pi.appendEntry() for durable parent-child tree (used by rf0 resumption, xal list), pi.sendMessage() for inline transcript events (used by 674 renderer). Two channels, two purposes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- thread_meta written as first custom entry in child session after the session header — marks it as a thread session
- thread_spawned written to parent session via pi.appendEntry() for durable tree
- thread_spawned also emitted via pi.sendMessage() with customType for inline transcript rendering
- thread_completed written on finish via SessionManager child-side; single entry type with status field covering completed/error/aborted/closed
- No separate thread_closed entry type — 'closed' is a status value on thread_completed
- InterAgentCommunication envelope prepended to child prompts as text: '[From <author> to <recipient>]: <content>'
- All transcript message types registered via pi.registerMessageRenderer for inline TUI rendering
- All types exported from shared types.ts
- SessionManager.list() + scan entries for customType==='thread_meta' correctly identifies thread sessions among regular sessions
close_reason: Persistence layer implemented: types, dual-write helpers, renderers, tests (8/8 passing)

title: ThreadManager: core thread lifecycle management
description:
Central manager that tracks active threads (Map<ThreadId, ChildProcess>), manages spawning, status tracking, and shutdown. Initializes on session_start by using SessionManager.list() to scan native Pi session directory (~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-hash>/) for thread sessions (identified by thread_meta custom entry) that are incomplete. Resumption logic is detailed separately in rf0.

## Acceptance Criteria
- ThreadManager class holds Map<ThreadId, { process, sessionFile, threadName, status }>
- spawn() uses SessionManager.create() in native Pi sessions dir, spawns pi subprocess, returns thread metadata
- wait() polls child session via SessionManager for thread_completed entry, returns result
- list() uses SessionManager.list() + filters for thread_meta entries to return threads with status
- interrupt() kills subprocess and marks thread aborted in its session
- close() writes thread_completed entry and marks as archived
- getStatusFeed() returns recent activity excerpts from running subprocess stdout streams
- On session_start, delegates to resumption logic (rf0) to find and rebuild incomplete threads
- Thread sessions live in same directory as regular Pi sessions — distinguished by thread_meta custom entry
close_reason: ThreadManager lifecycle: spawn/wait/interrupt/close/getStatusFeed/resume, 25 tests, spawn race fixed

title: wait_thread tool: block until subagent completes
description:
Register a wait_thread tool that: 1) Supports waiting on a single thread_id OR array of thread_ids 2) Polls each thread's session file for completion 3) Streams progress via onUpdate with per-thread status 4) Resolves with final output(s) when all waited threads complete or any error 5) Emits SubAgentActivityEvent (kind: Interacted) on start of wait. Support timeout parameter per thread.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wait accepts { thread_ids: string[], timeout?: number }
- Emits 'Waiting for <name>: Running' inline transcript event on start
- Polls all waited threads every 500ms checking for thread_completed entries
- Calls onUpdate with current status: { waiting: [{ name, status, lastActivity }] }
- Resolves when all threads complete with aggregated outputs
- Times out with error if timeout exceeded (per thread or global)
- Emits 'Finished waiting -> <name>: <status>' on resolution
- Live status feed widget updates during wait
close_reason: wait_thread complete; live widget deferred to pi-threads-ej8

title: spawn_thread tool: create subagent thread with isolated context
description:
Register a spawn_thread tool that: 1) Creates a new persistent session via SessionManager.create() in the native Pi sessions directory 2) Writes thread_meta as first custom entry after session header with parent_id, thread_name, depth, task, agent_type 3) Wraps initial prompt in InterAgentCommunication envelope so child LLM knows the message came from a peer agent 4) Spawns pi subprocess pointing at the session file (--session flag) 5) Writes thread_spawned via both channels: pi.appendEntry() for durable parent-child tree AND pi.sendMessage(customType: 'thread_spawned') for inline TUI transcript 6) Returns thread_id and thread_name. Supports optional --model and --tools overrides via child CLI flags. Context forking (fork_turns) is optional — default is 'none' (fresh context).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tool accepts: task, thread_name, agent_type, model?, tools?, fork_turns?, cwd?
- Creates session via SessionManager.create() in native sessions dir (~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-hash>/)
- First custom entry in child session after header is thread_meta with parent context
- Initial prompt wrapped in InterAgentCommunication: '[From root to <thread_name>]: <task>'
- Spawns pi subprocess with --session, --model, --tools flags (matching pi-fork runner.ts pattern)
- Subprocess is spawned with pi-threads extension loaded so child-side poll loop works for send_to_thread
- Returns { thread_id, thread_name } to parent agent
- thread_spawned written via pi.appendEntry() for durable tree + pi.sendMessage() with customType 'thread_spawned' for inline transcript
- Parent session tree entries (appendEntry) are separate from inline events (sendMessage) — two channels, two purposes
- fork_turns defaults to 'none'; context forking is an optional enhancement (pi-threads-65k)
close_reason: spawn_thread AC verified: SessionManager.create, thread_meta, dual-write, subprocess with extension

title: close_thread tool: clean shutdown of a completed thread
description:
Register a close_thread tool (distinct from interrupt_thread) that cleanly marks a thread as closed/archived. Writes or updates thread_completed entry with status: 'closed' to the child session. Updates parent session with SubAgentActivityEvent (kind: Closed) via pi.sendMessage. Thread's session file is retained for audit/history but marked as terminal and hidden from default list_threads output. Uses the existing thread_completed entry type — no separate thread_closed entry.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tool accepts { thread_id }
- Only works on threads that are already completed (thread_completed entry exists)
- Updates thread_completed status to 'closed' (or writes new thread_completed with status 'closed' if none exists)
- Emits 'Closed <name>' inline transcript event via pi.sendMessage customType 'thread_closed'
- SubAgentActivityEvent(Closed) written to parent session
- Thread no longer appears in list_threads default output (archived = thread_completed.status === 'closed')
- Error if thread is still running (use interrupt_thread instead)
close_reason: close_thread: requires completed, writes closed status, transcript, hidden from default list

title: Transcript inline events: render spawn/send/wait in parent chat
description:
Use pi.sendMessage() with custom message types to inject inline transcript events into the parent's message stream. Rendered as formatted blocks mimicking Codex: • Spawned <name> [agent_type] + task preview • Sent input to <name> + message preview • Waiting for <name>: <status> → Finished waiting: <status>. Register a message renderer so these appear inline with assistant output, not just in tool result panels.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Custom message types: thread_spawned, thread_send, thread_wait, thread_interrupted, thread_closed
- Spawned renders as '• Spawned <name> [<agent_type>]' with task preview
- Sent renders as '• Sent input to <name>' with message preview
- Wait renders as '• Waiting for <name>: Running' updating to '• Finished waiting → <name>: Completed'
- Interrupted renders as '• Interrupted <name>'
- Closed renders as '• Closed <name>'
- Messages appear inline in TUI via registerMessageRenderer
- All transcript events have appropriate theme colors (success/warning/error/muted)
close_reason: Transcript inline renderers for all 5 types with theme colors

title: Live status feed widget: real-time subagent activity preview
description:
Implement a ctx.ui.setWidget() feed that shows up to 3 lines of recent activity from running subagents while the parent waits. Parses child subprocess JSON-RPC stdout lines (the pi-fork processPiJsonLine pattern — NOT Codex's ThreadBufferedEvent) to extract tool_call summaries: 'read <path>', '$ <command>', 'edit <path>', 'grep <pattern>'. Displays indented beneath each thread's name with a live status indicator (⏳ running / ✓ done / ✗ error).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Widget shows 'Sub-agents running' header when threads are active
- Each running thread shows: thread_name, status indicator, up to 3 recent activity lines
- Activity lines extracted from child subprocess stdout JSON lines (tool_call events): 'read <path>', '$ <command>', 'edit <path>', 'grep <pattern>'
- Widget updates in real-time as ThreadManager receives and parses child stdout
- Widget auto-hides when no threads are running
- Widget placed via ctx.ui.setWidget() with appropriate placement
- Works during wait_thread execution (visible to user while parent is blocked)
close_reason: Live status feed widget during wait_thread via setWidget

title: Resumption: resume thread tree on session_start
description:
On session_start, ThreadManager uses SessionManager.list() to find all sessions in the native Pi session directory (~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-hash>/). For each session, opens it and scans its entries for any entry with customType === 'thread_meta'. Threads with thread_meta but no thread_completed are incomplete — respawn their pi subprocess pointing at the session file. Threads with both thread_meta and thread_completed are terminal (skip). Rebuilds parent-child tree from thread_meta entries and parent session thread_spawned entries. Does not respawn threads with explicit error/abort markers.

## Acceptance Criteria
- On session_start, calls SessionManager.list() to enumerate all sessions
- Opens each session and scans all entries for customType === 'thread_meta' (may not be literally first if entry ordering changes)
- Incomplete: has thread_meta, no thread_completed → respawn pi --session <path>
- Terminal: has both thread_meta and thread_completed → skip
- Error/abort: thread_completed with status 'error' or 'aborted' → skip (do not respawn)
- Rebuilds ThreadManager active map with resumed threads
- Resumed threads continue from last message in their session file
- Cross-references parent session thread_spawned entries (via pi.appendEntry) to rebuild tree structure
close_reason: session_start resumption: filter incomplete, skip terminal, tree rebuild, resume without prompt

title: interrupt_thread tool: force-stop a running thread
description:
Register an interrupt_thread tool that: 1) Kills the subprocess for a running thread via SIGTERM/SIGKILL 2) Marks it as aborted in the session 3) Emits SubAgentActivityEvent (kind: Interrupted). Distinct from close_thread (interrupt = force stop, close = clean archive after completion).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tool accepts { thread_id }
- Sends SIGTERM to thread subprocess; escalates to SIGKILL after 5s
- Writes thread_completed entry with status: 'aborted' to child session
- Emits 'Interrupted <name>' inline transcript event
- SubAgentActivityEvent(Interrupted) written to parent session
- Removes thread from ThreadManager active map
- Error if thread is not running
close_reason: interrupt_thread: SIGTERM/SIGKILL, aborted completion, transcript, map removal

title: send_to_thread tool: inject message into running thread
description:
Register a send_to_thread tool that: 1) Wraps message in InterAgentCommunication envelope (author=parent, recipient=thread_name) 2) Appends as user message entry into the target thread's session file via SessionManager 3) Child subprocess detects new messages via periodic polling of its own session file (configurable interval, default 2s) 4) Emits SubAgentActivityEvent (kind: Interacted) and inline transcript event. Enables ongoing parent-child communication.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tool accepts { thread_id, message }
- Wraps message: '[From root to <thread_name>]: <message>' as user message content
- Appends user message entry to child session via SessionManager
- Child subprocess is spawned with pi-threads extension loaded (or equivalent poll loop) so message detection works
- Child detection: subprocess polls its own session file every 2s for new entries after its last processed entry
- Emits 'Sent input to <name>' inline transcript event via pi.sendMessage customType 'thread_send'
- SubAgentActivityEvent(Interacted) written to parent session via pi.appendEntry()
- Error if thread_id does not exist or thread is not running
- Polling interval is configurable; polling stops when thread_completed is detected
close_reason: send_to_thread with child poll loop, dual-write, envelope

title: list_threads tool: enumerate active and completed threads
description:
Register a list_threads tool that uses SessionManager.list() with thread_meta filtering to enumerate all thread sessions. Shows: thread_id, thread_name, parent_id, depth, status (running/completed/error/aborted/closed), task summary, usage stats. Support filtering by status. Archived threads (thread_completed.status === 'closed') are hidden from default output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tool accepts { status?: 'running' | 'completed' | 'error' | 'aborted' | 'all' }
- Uses SessionManager.list() to find sessions, opens each to scan entries for thread_meta
- Returns array of thread summaries: thread_id, thread_name, parent_id, depth, status, task, usage, model
- Archived threads (thread_completed.status === 'closed') included only when status='all' or status='completed'
- Default shows active threads: running + completed/error/aborted (non-closed)
- Formatted for LLM consumption with clear structure
- Custom TUI rendering shows tree view indented by depth
close_reason: list_threads tool complete; TUI tree view deferred to pi-threads-u6x

title: Agent picker: switch between main agent and subagent transcripts
description:
Register a /threads command that lets the user cycle between the parent/main session and active subagent thread sessions. Maintains extension-owned navigation state (thread order list, current position) — NOT Codex's AgentNavigationState. Uses ctx.switchSession(path) (available on ExtensionCommandContext, so this must be a command, not a tool shortcut) to switch between distinct thread session files. Registers Alt+Left / Alt+Right keybindings via pi.registerShortcut(). Status bar shows current thread name.

## Acceptance Criteria
- /threads command lists all active thread sessions with status indicators
- Extension maintains its own ordered list of thread session paths for stable navigation
- Alt+Left/Alt+Right registered via pi.registerShortcut() cycles through list
- Switching uses ctx.switchSession(path) — correct Pi API for distinct session files (NOT navigateTree, which is for branch navigation within one session)
- Command handler runs on ExtensionCommandContext, giving access to session-switching APIs
- Status bar shows current thread: 'Main' or '<thread_name> [<agent_type>]' via ctx.ui.setStatus()
- Navigation state cached; threads that complete remain in cycle until user navigates away
- Uses safe session replacement lifecycle (withSession callback pattern from docs/extensions.md)
close_reason: /threads command, session navigation, status bar, Alt+Left/Right via commands

title: Context forking: copy parent context into child thread
description:
Support fork_turns parameter on spawn_thread: none (fresh context), all (full parent history), or N (last N turns). Implement by reading parent session entries and injecting as context messages into child session.

## Acceptance Criteria
- fork_turns: none creates child with empty context (only InterAgentCommunication message)
- fork_turns: all copies entire parent history as context before the initial task
- fork_turns: N copies the last N turns (user+assistant pairs) from parent
- Copied context preserves tool calls and results
- Context messages are inserted before the InterAgentCommunication initial prompt
close_reason: fork_turns wired into spawn: none/all/N with integration test

title: TUI rendering: custom expandable thread display
description:
Custom renderCall and renderResult for thread tools. Collapsed view shows status icon + thread ID + task preview. Expanded view (Ctrl+O) shows full task, all tool calls, final output as Markdown, and usage stats.

## Acceptance Criteria
- renderCall shows tool invocation with agent_type, task preview, and scope
- renderResult collapsed: status icon (✓/✗/⏳) + thread name + last 5-10 items + usage stats
- renderResult expanded: full task, all tool calls with formatted args (bash/read/edit/grep style), final output as Markdown, per-thread usage
- spawn_thread result shows thread_id and initial preview
- wait_thread result shows all waited threads with status and outputs
- list_threads result shows tree view (indented by depth) with status icons
- All views use appropriate theme colors
close_reason: Custom expandable tool TUI rendering for all 6 tools, error path fixed
